purpose: Seventh AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering the completed self-target command-buffer init slice that dispatches proc-l-init.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular self command-buffer init node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular self command-buffer init node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular self command-buffer init node.

layers.summary: A matching one-hot standard input completes buffer 00101, decodes it as self/proc-l-init, and reconfigures the stem node into processor-left.
layers.boundary: This layer states only the self-target init-family command-buffer slice; it does not execute neighbor routing or self-target non-init commands.

layers.summary: The self command-buffer init node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one completed self-target init buffer dispatch.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0037 command-buffer self-init subset and adds no neighbor delivery or write-buffer execution.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested command-buffer dispatch.
